factors.name: US Labor Market Weakness (NFP)
factors.summary: Thursday's softer-than-expected nonfarm payrolls data has reduced Fed rate-hike expectations, particularly for September, lowering near-term real yields. This is typically supportive for long-duration growth stocks like mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Fed Policy Repricing
factors.summary: Market now pricing ~50% odds of a November rate hike (vs higher expectations prior), and doubts on September hikes have deepened. Lower real yields benefit NDX-heavy tech names with long cash-flow durations.

factors.name: Macro Momentum & Risk-On
factors.summary: Global stocks hit historic all-time highs pre-holiday; Bitcoin at $62.3K; AUD, GBP, NZD, EUR all strengthening on USD weakness post-NFP. Risk appetite intact despite heat wave/power grid tensions.

factors.name: US Power Grid Stress & Heat Wave
factors.summary: Extreme heat threatening US power grids and forcing emergency curbs; could disrupt Q3 earnings/operations and cap upside momentum into late summer. Electricity supply risk may weigh on data center / AI capex near-term.

factors.name: Geopolitical Calm (Iran-US Deal)
factors.summary: OPEC output surge, Strait of Hormuz reopening, and US-Iran talks reducing energy shock fears. Lower oil (~$68.65 WTI) reduces inflation pressure and supports equities; gold weakness also aligns with lower real yields benefiting equities.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Window
factors.summary: No major FAANG earnings in news window; Q2 reporting season largely concluded. Next catalysts are earnings guidance/conference calls and capital allocation announcements (e.g., CPP's $1.75B EQT AI infrastructure deal signals continued capex appetite).

factors.name: AI & Semiconductor Sentiment
factors.summary: Modest AI bullishness (Kleiner Perkins VC outlook, robotics hype) but no major semiconductor supply or demand shocks; Zcash/crypto noise unrelated to NDX.

factors.name: USD Weakness Post-NFP
factors.summary: USD/JPY, USD/CHF, USD/CNH all weakening on softer labor data. Weaker dollar reduces headwinds for NQ international revenues and typically supports equity multiples.

factors.name: Existing Move (4h: +0.44%, 24h: -1.24%)
factors.summary: NAS100 flat to modestly positive in quiet US holiday trading (Independence Day). Bounce from -1.24% 24h low is underway but move is small and lacks conviction; holiday liquidity is thin.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: Softer NFP should push real yields lower (5Y TIPS expected to compress), directly bullish for long-duration growth; no competing inflation surprise to extend nominal yields sharply higher.

screening.narrative: NAS100 opened Q3 mixed after a blockbuster H1, with Thursday's weaker-than-expected NFP data triggering a dovish repricing of Fed policy (Sept hike odds now ~50% vs prior bullish bets). This has supported equities by pushing real yields lower, benefiting long-duration mega-cap tech. Geopolitical risks have eased (Iran-US deal, OPEC output surge) and global risk appetite remains intact at all-time highs. However, the move is unfolding in thin, holiday-shortened US trading, and a US power grid stress event from an extreme heat wave adds a near-term operational wildcard. The bias is cautiously bullish on fundamentals (lower real yields, dovish pivot) but tempered by phase-2 consolidation and lack of fresh mega-cap catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Minutes (likely early–mid week, will clarify Sept hike intentions)
Mega-cap earnings guidance / capital allocation (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL guidance windows vary; check earnings calendar)
10Y Treasury auction (next week; will test real yield floor)
CPI / PCE inflation data (mid-month; biggest NDX driver if surprise occurs)
FOMC meeting (likely July 29–30; will reset rate expectations)
screening.risk_warning: If another hot inflation print (CPI, PCE) arrives mid-month, or if Fed speakers push back on dovish repricing, real yields could re-steepen and crush NDX multiples. Power grid failures or broader macro disruption from heat wave could trigger liquidity crisis / circuit breakers. Also: thin holiday liquidity can reverse moves sharply; watch for whipsaw into re-open (Tuesday).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early phase-2 consolidation; avoid chasing up into resistance 29,450–29,600. Wait for retest of 29,100–29,150 support zone with bullish divergence (lower lows on volume) or break above 29,600 with conviction before committing. Holiday liquidity is thin—size down and expect slippage.

factors.name: NFP surprise (Thursday)
factors.summary: Softer-than-expected US jobs data released Thursday weakened USD and reduced Fed hike odds into September. This typically supports growth equities and multiples, but market has already priced initial relief.

factors.name: Real yields / nominal yield trajectory
factors.summary: No new Treasury data in news window. Gold rallied >1% on weaker NFP and is up $53 to $4,147, signaling lower real yields, but reaction appears 1 day old. Monitor 10Y yield for fresh directional clue.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings cycle status
factors.summary: No earnings surprises or guidance revisions reported in last 24h. Q2 earnings season is winding down; major drivers (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL) already reported. Forward catalysts unclear.

factors.name: AI capex and hyperscaler commentary
factors.summary: Mention of CPP investing $1.75B in EQT's AI infrastructure and Nvidia robotics thesis, but no new earnings calls or capex guidance in this window. Sentiment remains constructive but no fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply chain
factors.summary: SK Hynix ADR offering and Chile lithium expansion reported, but no supply shock or demand cliff signal. No TSMC/ASML/Samsung production warnings.

factors.name: USD weakness
factors.summary: Dollar weakened sharply post-NFP; GBP +1% on week, AUD, NZD, EUR all rallied. Weaker USD typically bullish for NAS100 (international revenue tailwind), though move is 1 day old.

factors.name: VIX / risk sentiment
factors.summary: Global stocks hit all-time highs pre-holiday (Dow, market cap records noted). Bitcoin rallied to $62.3K nine-day high. Risk-on tone supportive, but muted by US Independence Day holiday (thin trading).

factors.name: China macro / geopolitics
factors.summary: China PMI resilient per Societe Generale; Yuan extending gains. Coast Guard Taiwan patrol rotation noted but no escalation. No chip export control tightening reported.

factors.name: Fed policy / rate expectations
factors.summary: Market pricing 50% odds of BOE hike in November and doubts Fed will hike in September post-NFP. Lower-for-longer narrative supports long-duration growth multiples if sustained.

factors.name: Holiday trading and volatility
factors.summary: US markets closed July 4; Asia/Europe trading thin. NFP relief rally from Thursday may reverse or consolidate on low volume. Avoid chasing in thin holiday sessions.

screening.narrative: NAS100 has bounced modestly (+0.44% in 4h) off Thursday's softer NFP print, which triggered a broad risk-on rebound and USD weakness. Global equities hit all-time highs; Bitcoin rallied to 9-day highs; gold broke $4,147. However, the move is 1+ days old and in thin holiday trading (US closed for Independence Day). Tech earnings cycle is winding down with no major guidance surprises in the news window, and hyperscaler capex commentary is stale. The bias remains constructively tilted on lower real yields and dovish Fed repricing, but conviction is muted by extended move and lack of fresh catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: US markets reopen post-July 4 — watch opening gaps and breadth into Tuesday
Fed minutes (timing TBD) — may clarify September rate hold conviction
CPI, PPI prints next week — key inflation test; any surprise hot could reverse rally
PCE personal spending data — household spending trends amid 'stressed consumer' narrative (General Mills warning)
FOMC meeting minutes release — transparency on labor-market and inflation debate
Mega-cap earnings revisions and guidance updates from Q2 season close-out
Treasury 10Y yield move — critical for tech multiple re-rating; watch 4.0–4.2% range
screening.risk_warning: If CPI or PCE data surprise hot next week, or if Fed minutes signal hawkish pivot (rejecting market's September hold pricing), the NFP-driven relief rally will reverse and real yields will spike, hitting long-duration Nasdaq names hard. Conversely, if 10Y yields break below 4.0%, upside could accelerate to 29,800+. Holiday thin volume makes stops unreliable; avoid aggressive leverage until US market reopens. China slowdown (PMI resilience but structural demand risks) or geopolitical flare (Taiwan, Middle East) could trigger risk-off repositioning.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is constructively tilted but move is extended (1+ day old in thin holiday session). On reopening, await retest of 29,150–29,250 support or a pullback from intraday highs before aggressive long entries. If CPI surprises, be prepared for sharp reversal to 28,900–29,000. Short entries only after failed break above 29,600 resistance; real-yield reacceleration or Fed hawkish signal would validate.
